title: SSC CGL 2026 Sectional Timing Tier I 30 30

questions.question: You are disheartened to find out that your friend was involved in a crime and your seniors are making an arrest warrant ready against him. Your friend has been facing some financial issues since last year and is the only caretaker of his parents. What should be your first reaction?
options: Alert your friend about the arrest warrant
Ask your friend to leave town along with his parents
Cooperate with your seniors in arresting him
Confront your friend about the crime and tell him that you are ashamed of what he has done
questions.explanation: As a maintainer of public order and peace, it is your duty to let go off bonds with friends and cooperate with your seniors in bringing justice and arresting criminals.

questions.question: Select the option that is related to the third term in the same way as the second term is related to the first term and sixth term is related to the fifth term. 12 : 42 :: 15 : ? :: 25 : 87.5
questions.explanation: Here, the pattern is: 12*3.5 = 42 25*3.5= 87.5 Similarly, 15*3.5= 52.5

questions.question: Study the following information carefully and answer the question given below. M%N: M is the husband of N M>N: M is the wife of N M*N: M is the mother of N M@N: M is the sister of N If C@A%G*D%F*B@E, then which of the following statements are incorrect? I: C is the grandfather of B II: F is the father of E III: B is the granddaughter of A
questions.explanation: C@A: C is the sister of A A%G: A is the husband of G G*D: G is the mother of D ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p29106/image005.png) D%F: D is the husband of F F*B: F is the mother of B B@E: B is the sister of E ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p29106/image006.png) I: C is the grandfather of B ----- False II: F is the father of E ----- False III: B is the granddaughter of A ------ True So, both I and II are incorrect.

questions.question: Consider the given statements to be true and decide which of the given conclusion(s) logically follow(s) from the statements. Statements: All Cherry is Apple. No Apple is Mango. Conclusions: I: Some Mangoes are Cherry. II: No Cherry is Mango
options: Both conclusions I and II follow
Neither conclusion I nor II follows
questions.explanation: Venn diagram: ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p24819/image008.jpg) I: Some Mangoes are Cherry ----- Does not follow II: No Cherry is Mango ----- Follow So, only conclusion II follows.

questions.question: Shoes are related to Legs in the same way Gloves are related to:
questions.explanation: As we wear Shoes in legs in the same way we wear Gloves in Hands.

questions.question: Select the correct combination of mathematical signs to replace the * signs and balance the given equation. 243 * 3 * 3 * 2 = 25
questions.explanation: Using the operators from option (A), the equation becomes 243 ÷ 3 ÷ 3 - 2 = 25. Evaluating from left to right: • 243 ÷ 3 = 81 • 81 ÷ 3 = 27 • 27 - 2 = 25 The equation is balanced.

questions.question: Three of the following four number-pairs are alike in a certain way and thus form a group. Which of the pair that does not belong to that group? (NOTE: Operations should be performed on the whole numbers, without breaking down the numbers into its constituent digits. E.g. 13 - Operations on 13 such as adding/ subtracting/ multiplying etc. to 13 can be performed. Breaking down 13 into 1 and 3 and then performing mathematical operations on 1 and 3 is not allowed.)

Three of the following word pairs are alike in some manner and hence form a group. Which word pair does not belong to that group? (The words must be considered as meaningful English words and must not be grouped based on the number of letters/number of consonants/vowels in the word)
questions.explanation: Option (a): Unit of Sound = Hertz Option (b): Unit of Height = Feet Option (c): Unit of Energy = Joule Option (d): Unit of Weight = Gram Except Energy, in all other options their units are given.

questions.question: Select the option that is related to the third term in the same way as the first term is related to the second term. 192/3 : 64 :: 455/5 : ?
The position of how many letters will remain unchanged if all the consonants followed by vowels in the word 'CONSTRUCTION' are arranged in English alphabetical order?
questions.explanation: CONSTRUCTION All the consonants followed by vowels in the word are arranged in English alphabetical order: CCNNRSTTIOOU So, the position of the three letters remain unchanged. Thus, option (b) is the correct answer.

questions.question: Select the option that is related to the third number in the same way as the second number is related to the first number. 56 : 30 :: 78 : ?
questions.explanation: (56 + 4) ÷ 2 = 60 ÷ 2 = 30 (78 + 4) ÷ 2 = 82 ÷ 2 = 41 So, option (a) is correct.

questions.question: Arrange the following in a logical and meaningful order. I) Form filling II) Admit card III) Result IV) Eligibility V) Exam
questions.explanation: The correct logical and meaningful order is Eligibility, Form filling, Admit card, Exam, and Result.

questions.question: Find the wrong term in the series given below. 277, 398, 569, 792, 1081

The question given below consists of a statement followed by two arguments numbered I and II. You have to decide which of the arguments is a 'strong' argument. Statement: Should India be allowed to host the next Olympics? Arguments: I. No, India lacks sufficient infrastructure for such a large-scale sports event. II. Yes, every country must be allowed to host Olympic games.
options: Both I and II are strong.
Both I and II are weak.
questions.explanation: The reasoning in statement I is strong as we can relate to the practical aspect of insufficient infrastructure being a hindrance to hosting a large-scale sporting event. It is not only practical but sufficiently answers the "why" aspect of the given statement. Whereas, in II, the reasoning is weak because it is a generic statement without sufficiently answering why every country must be allowed to host Olympic games. Therefore, the correct answer is option a).

questions.question: If 7 ⊕ 3 = 10 and 6 ⊕ 5 = 11, determine 8 ⊕ 6.
questions.explanation: Infer rule from the two true statements Assume A ⊕ B = A + B Check: 7 + 3 => 10 (matches), 6 + 5 => 11 (matches) Compute: 8 + 6 => 14

questions.question: Pointing towards a woman Arya said to Ruhan, "Her father is the only son of your mother." How is that woman related to Ruhan?
questions.explanation: Her father is the only son of your mother. ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p20975/image004.jpg) That woman is the Niece of Ruhan.

questions.question: A word is represented by only one set of numbers as given in any one of the alternatives. The sets of numbers given in the alternatives are represented by two classes of alphabets as shown in the two matrices. The columns and rows of Matrix-I are numbered from 0 to 4 and that of Matrix-II are numbered from 5 to 9. A letter from these matrices can be represented by its row and next by its column, for example, 'N' can be represented by 31, 14, etc., and 'I' can be represented by 57, 75, etc. Similarly, you have to identify the set for the word "MOIST". Matrix - I: 01234 0OCUNT 1COTUN 2NTCOU 3UNOTC 4TUNCO Matrix - II: 56789 5TSIEM 6EMSIT 7IETMS 8MTESI 9SIMTE

Six people in society were of different ages. R is elder to T but not immediate and is not the eldest. J is elder to at least three people. U is younger to T but elder to E. M is elder to J. Who among the following is elder to R?
questions.explanation: R is elder to T but is not the eldest. J is elder to at least three people. U is younger to T but elder to E. M is elder to J. So we get: M > R > J> T> U> E So M is elder to R.

questions.question: If all letters of the word 'TURBULENCE' are arranged in the alphabetical order from left to right without repetition, then which of the following will be the fourth letter from the left end?
questions.explanation: The given word is TURBULENCE. If all letters are arranged in the alphabetical order without repetition, then we get: BCELNRTU. So, fourth letter from left end will be L.

questions.question: Find the missing term in the following series - 617, 598, 560, 503, ?
questions.explanation: Logic for the given series - 617 - 19 = 598 598 - 38 = 560 560 - 57 = 503 503 - 76 = 427 Hence, missing term = 427

questions.question: Find the missing term(?) in the following series. 50, 20, 16, 19.2, ?
questions.explanation: Logic for given series: 50 X 0.4 = 20 20 X 0.8 = 16 16 X 1.2 = 19.2 19.2 X 1.6 = 30.72 Hence, the missing term = 30.72

questions.question: Select the letter-cluster from among the given options that can replace the question mark (?) in the following series: GQK, HJS, IRL, JKT, ?
questions.explanation: G → H → I → J So, next first letter = J + 1 = K Q → J → R → K Q to J = -7 J to R = +8 R to K = -7 So, next second letter = K + 8 = S K → S → L → T K to S = +8 S to L = -7 L to T = +8 So, next third letter = T - 7 = M Required letter-cluster = KSM

questions.question: Rock earns Rs. 120 in a day. In a month of 31 days, he did not work for a week. What is the total amount earned by Rock in that month (in Rs)?
questions.explanation: The number of days for which Rock worked in that month = 31 - 7 = 24. So, the total amount earned by Rock in that month = 120 x 24 = Rs. 2880

questions.question: Meera starts from point O, walks 60 m south, then turns left and walks 80 m, turns left again and walks 30 m, then turns right and walks 50 m. In which direction is she from O now?
questions.explanation: Given conditions, From south, a left turn means east; from east, a left turn means north; from north, a right turn means east. ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p30245/image001.jpg) So, Meera is in South-East direction from O now.

questions.question: If in an examination the centre codes and roll numbers are formed by the initial of the State/UT followed by five digits in a fixed pattern (for example, MH28473 for Maharashtra), then which of the following roll numbers is most likely to be from Uttar Pradesh?
questions.explanation: Pattern = State initial + same five-digit number. Given example: Maharashtra → MH28473 For Uttar Pradesh, correct state code is UP, and digits remain 28473. So UP28473 is correct.

questions.question: What do we mean by Allen's rule?
options: Desert lizards lack the physiological ability that mammals have to deal with the high temperatures of their habitat.
Some plants have no leaves - they are reduced to spines - and the photosynthetic function is taken over by the flattened stems.
Mammals from colder climates generally have shorter ears and limbs to minimise heat loss.
The body compensates low oxygen availability by increasing red blood cell production, decreasing the binding affinity of haemoglobin and by increasing the breathing rate.
questions.explanation: Allen's rule is an ecological principle that describes how animal body shape adapts to different climates. Specifically, it focuses on how animals in colder regions have evolved physical characteristics that help them conserve body heat. Key points about Allen's rule: It explains why mammals in cold regions tend to have: o Shorter ears o Shorter limbs o More compact body shapes These adaptations help reduce surface area, which minimizes heat loss This is an example of how organisms adapt to their environmental conditions through evolutionary processes

questions.question: Which of the following river flow its longest course through Assam?
questions.explanation: The Brahmaputra, one of the major rivers in India, originates in the Angsi glacier of the Himalayas in Tibet. There it is known as the Tsangpo River. It enters India in Arunachal Pradesh and is called the Dihang River. It is joined by many other tributaries; the Dibang, the Lohit, and the Kenula to form the main Brahmaputra River and flow its longest course through Assam, enter Bangladesh and finally fall into the Bay of Bengal. The Brahmaputra has the greatest volume of water of all the rivers in India.

questions.question: Which of the following is correct about distance and displacement?
options: Displacement is always greater than Distance
Displacement can't be negative while Distance can be negative or zero
Displacement can be negative, positive or zero while distance can be positive
Displacement is a scalar quantity while Distance is a vector quantity
questions.explanation: • Displacement is a vector quantity; it can be negative, positive or zero while distance can be positive because it is a scalar quantity. • Distance is always greater or equal than displacement. Further Insight: AspectDistanceDisplacement DefinitionTotal path length traveled by an object.Shortest straight-line path between initial and final positions. Type of QuantityScalar (only magnitude).Vector (both magnitude and direction). MeasurementAlways positive or zero.Can be positive, negative, or zero depending on direction. Path DependenceDepends on the actual path taken.Independent of the path, depends only on initial and final positions. Value ComparisonEqual to or greater than displacement.Equal to or less than distance. ExampleWalking 10 km around a park (distance is 10 km).Starting and ending at the same point (displacement is 0).

questions.question: Which of the following statements are correct about Government of India Act, 1935? (i) The act abolished dyarchy in provinces (ii) Sindh was separated from Punjab (iii) The act provided for establishment of Reserve Bank of India (iv) Bihar and Orissa were split
questions.explanation: - Some key points about Government of India Act, 1935 (i) Introduced provincial autonomy (ii) Abolished dyarchy in provinces (iii) Sindh was separated from Bombay (iv) The act provided for establishment of Reserve Bank of India (v) Bihar and Orissa were split. (vi) Burma was completely separated from India

questions.question: Consider the following statements regarding India's achievements at the Olympic Games: A. The Indian men's hockey team has won a total of 8 gold medals at the Summer Olympics, with their first victory occurring in 1928. B. Neeraj Chopra's gold medal win at the 2020 Tokyo Olympics was India's first-ever Olympic medal in any track and field event. Which of the above statements is/are correct?
questions.explanation: Statement A is correct. The Indian men's hockey team has an illustrious Olympic history, winning 8 gold medals in total. Their first gold medal was indeed won at the 1928 Amsterdam Olympics. Statement B is incorrect. While Neeraj Chopra's gold was India's first gold medal in a track and field event, it was not the country's first medal. Norman Pritchard, representing India in the 1900 Paris Olympics, won two silver medals in athletics (in the 200m sprint and 200m hurdles).

questions.question: Match List-I and List-II regarding the organisms and the animal kingdom it belongs to. List-I(Organism)List-II(Animal Kingdom) a.Earthwormi.Nematoda b.Wuchereriaii.Annelida c.Asteriasiii.Echinodermata d.Unioiv.Mollusca
questions.explanation: Earthworm belongs to Annelida. Wuchereria (a genus of parasitic nematodes) belongs to Nematoda. Asterias (starfish) belong to Echinodermata. Unio (a genus of freshwater mussels) belongs to Mollusca. Annelida (Earthworm) • General Characteristics: Annelids are segmented worms, characterized by their elongated bodies divided into ring-like segments (metamerism). They have a true coelom (body cavity) and a well-developed organ system. • Habitat: Most annelids are found in aquatic environments, but many, like earthworms, inhabit moist soil. • Respiration: Respiration occurs through the skin in terrestrial forms or through gills in aquatic forms. • Reproduction: Annelids can reproduce sexually (most have both male and female organs) and asexually through fragmentation. • Examples: Earthworms (Lumbricus), leeches (Hirudo), and marine worms (Polychaeta). Nematoda (Wuchereria) • General Characteristics: Nematodes, or roundworms, are unsegmented, cylindrical worms with a complete digestive system. They have a tough outer cuticle and a pseudocoelom (a body cavity not entirely lined with mesoderm). • Habitat: Nematodes can be found in various habitats, including soil, freshwater, and marine environments. Some are parasitic. • Reproduction: Most nematodes reproduce sexually, and many species have complex life cycles. • Importance: Some nematodes are beneficial for soil health, while others, like Wuchereria, are parasites causing diseases such as lymphatic filariasis. • Examples: Caenorhabditis elegans (a model organism), Ascaris (intestinal roundworm), and Wuchereria bancrofti (causes lymphatic filariasis). Echinodermata (Asterias) • General Characteristics: Echinoderms are marine animals characterized by their radial symmetry (typically pentaradial), a calcareous endoskeleton, and a water vascular system that aids in locomotion and feeding. • Habitat: Exclusively marine, found in a range of environments from shallow waters to deep sea. • Unique Features: They possess tube feet operated by the water vascular system, allowing them to move, grasp food, and exchange gases. • Regeneration: Many echinoderms have remarkable regenerative abilities, allowing them to regrow lost body parts. • Examples: Starfish (Asterias), sea urchins (Echinoidea), and sea cucumbers (Holothuroidea). Mollusca (Unio) • General Characteristics: Mollusks are a diverse group with soft bodies, most of which are protected by a hard shell made of calcium carbonate. They have a true coelom and a complete digestive system. • Body Plan: The typical body plan includes three main parts: the muscular foot, the visceral mass (containing internal organs), and the mantle (which secretes the shell). • Habitat: Mollusks inhabit a variety of environments, including marine, freshwater, and terrestrial ecosystems. • Reproduction: Mollusks may reproduce sexually, and many have complex life cycles, including larval stages. • Examples: Bivalves (e.g., Unio), gastropods (e.g., snails, slugs), and cephalopods (e.g., squids, octopuses).

questions.question: With reference to 'Gotipua dance' consider the following statements and select the correct option. a. In Odia language, "Goti" means "single" and "Pua" means "boy". b. The dance is performed by young boys who dress up as female. c. It is performed to praise Lord Jagannath and Lord Krishna.
questions.explanation: In Odia language, "Goti" means "single" and "Pua" means "boy". For centuries, the Gotipua dance has been performed in Orissa by young boys who dress up as female to praise Lord Jagannath and Lord Krishna. The actual form of the dance is executed by a group of boys who perform acrobatic figures inspired by the life of Radha & Krishna. Further insights: Appearance & Costume: Young boys transform into female dancers, keeping their hair long to style with flowers. They wear elaborate makeup, including Kajal (eyeliner), Bindi (red dot), and traditional face paintings. The traditional dress, a "Kanchula" (blouse) and "pattasari" (waist cloth), is often replaced by modern, easier-to-wear costumes. Dancers wear special jewelry, ankle bells, and paint their hands and feet with red "Alta". These items are considered sacred. History & Style: The Gotipua tradition began in 16th-century Orissa when boys took over the temple dance roles from the declining female "Mahari" dancers. It is considered the precursor to the modern Odissi dance style, and many Odissi masters were once Gotipua dancers. Unlike other forms, the dancers do their own singing. The style is known for two basic postures: "Tribhangi" (a body pose with three bends) and "Chouka" (a square-like stance).

questions.question: Under the NIRVIK Scheme (Niryat Rin Vikas Yojana), which of the following is not a key objective?
options: Simplifying loan procedures for small exporters
questions.explanation: • The NIRVIK Scheme focuses on insurance coverage and simplifying credit, but it does not offer direct capital subsidies. Further Insight: • NIRVIK Scheme (Niryat Rin Vikas Yojana) • Launch Date: February 1, 2020 • Aim: Improve export credit availability and provide insurance to exporters. • Main Features: ○ Provides insurance coverage up to 90% of the loan amount. ○ Reduces insurance premium rates for small exporters. ○ Ensures quicker and easier loan disbursal to exporters. ○ Focus on promoting Indian exports globally.

questions.question: Which of the following statements is/are true? I. Chhath Puja is a major Hindu festival celebrated in Bihar, Jharkhand, and Uttar Pradesh. II. It is dedicated to the Sun God (Surya) and his wife, Usha.
questions.explanation: Chhath Puja is a major Hindu festival celebrated in Bihar, Jharkhand, and Uttar Pradesh, dedicated to the Sun God (Surya) and his wife, Usha. It involves rituals performed at sunrise and sunset to thank the Sun God for sustaining life and granting wishes.

questions.question: Match the following elements of political life with their features in the Later Vedic Period: Column 1Column 2 (i) Rajasuya(a) Royal sacrifice to establish sovereignty (ii) Ashvamedha(b) Strengthened monarchy through ritual (iii) Sabha(c) Council of elders advising the king (iv) Samiti(d) Assembly of people with declining importance
questions.explanation: • Rajasuya: A royal ritual aimed at asserting sovereignty. • Ashvamedha: A horse sacrifice symbolizing territorial power. • Sabha: Council of elders • Samiti: Assembly of people with declining influence in the Later Vedic Period

questions.question: Consider the following statements: I. In this Classical dance the artist wears a special costume called 'Kumil', an elaborate barrel shaped long skirt. II. Here a barrel drum called 'Pung' is played as the musical instrument. Which of the following is the Classical dance mentioned above?
questions.explanation: Kumil is a special barrel shaped long skirt worn by the artist. Pung is a bamboo made barrel drum. The dance in Manipur is associated with rituals and traditional festivals, there are legendary references to the dances of Shiva and Parvati and other gods and goddesses who created the universe. With the arrival of Vaishnavism in the 15th century A.D., new compositions based on episodes from the life of Radha and Krishna were gradually introduced. Source: CCRT website Classical dances of India

questions.question: Identify the true statement about chess. a. On its first move, a pawn has the option to move one or two squares forward. b. Bishop moves only diagonally across the board. c. Castling is a special move in chess that allows a player to move the king and one of the rooks simultaneously under specific conditions.
questions.explanation: a. On its first move, a pawn has the option to move one or two squares forward. This is a correct statement. From its starting square, a pawn can advance either one or two squares. It captures diagonally. b. Bishop moves only diagonally across the board. This is also a correct statement. The bishop's movement is restricted to diagonal lines. c. Castling is a special move in chess that allows a player to move the king and one of the rooks simultaneously under specific conditions. This is also a correct statement. It accurately describes the special move of castling.

questions.question: Which of the following statements about Veda Samaj is correct? I. It was established in Madras in 1864. II. It worked to abolish caste distinctions and promote widow remarriage and women's education.
questions.explanation: Statement I is correct: Veda Samaj was founded or established by Keshab Chandra Sen and K. Sridharalu Naidu in Madras in 1864. Veda Samaj was inspired by Brahmo Samaj and it is sometimes called as the Brahmo Samaj of South India. Statement II is correct: Veda Samaj advocated eliminating caste distinctions and promoting widow remarriage and girl education.

questions.question: Who was elected as the 37th President of the Board of Control for Cricket in India (BCCI) in 2025?
questions.explanation: Mithun Manhas, the former Delhi captain, formally took over as the 37th president of the Board of Control for Cricket in India (BCCI) during its 94th Annual General Meeting (AGM) in Mumbai on September 28, 2025, succeeding Roger Binny and becoming the first former cricketer in the top post since Sourav Ganguly. He was elected unopposed, marking a significant transition for Indian cricket administration.

questions.question: Which of the following musical instruments is/are type of 'Sushira Vadya (instrument)'? I. Flute II. Santoor III. Drums Select the answer using the below codes.
questions.explanation: In the Sushira Vadya (wind instrument) group, sound is produced by blowing air into a hollow column. The pitch of the note is determined by controlling the air passage and the melody is played by using the fingers to open and close the in the instrument. The simplest of these instruments is the flute. Santoor is 'Tata Vadya (string instrument)'. The tata Vadya is a category of instruments in which sound is produced by the vibration of a string or chord. Drums are 'Avanaddha Vadya'. In the Avanaddha Vadya category of instruments, sound is produced by striking the animal skin which has been stretched across an earthen or metal pot or a wooden barrel or frame. Source- CCRT website

questions.question: Identify which of the following transactions are included in India's Current Account of Balance of Payments (BoP)? I. Export and import of goods II. Export and import of services III. Transfer payments IV. Sales of assets Answer Codes:
options: All I, II, III and IV
questions.explanation: The Balance of Payments (BoP) records the transactions in goods, services and assets between residents of a country with the rest of the world for a specified time period typically a year. There are two main accounts in the BoP - the current account and the capital account. The Current account records exports and imports in goods and services and transfer payments. The Capital account records all international purchases and sales of assets such as money, stocks, bonds, etc.

questions.question: The main objective of the 12th Five-Year Plan is
options: Sustainable and inclusive growth to reduce unemployment.
Faster, sustainable and more inclusive growth.
questions.explanation: 12th Five-Year Plan was implemented between 2012-17 and was the last five-year plan in India. It included in its objective the slogan of "Faster, sustainable and more inclusive growth", recognizing the need for reducing inequality and making the growth more sustainable.

questions.question: Match the Following list of 2025 Nobel Prize Winners in List I with their Major Contribution in List II. List I List II A. John Clarke 1. Demonstration of macroscopic quantum-mechanical tunnelling and energy quantisation in electric circuits B. Omar M. Yaghi 2. Development of porous crystalline metal-organic frameworks (MOFs) for gas storage and catalysis C. Mary E. Brunkow 3. Discoveries about peripheral immune tolerance in the human immune system D. Maria Corina Machado 4. Promoting democratic rights and leading a peaceful transition effort in Venezuela
questions.explanation: John Clarke (Physics) - John Clarke, along with Michel H. Devoret and John M. Martinis, was awarded the 2025 Nobel Prize in Physics "for the discovery of macroscopic quantum mechanical tunnelling and energy quantisation in an electric circuit" Omar M. Yaghi, along with Susumu Kitagawa and Richard Robson, won the 2025 Nobel Prize in Chemistry for inventing Metal-Organic Frameworks (MOFs), revolutionary porous materials built from metal nodes and organic linkers, enabling huge internal spaces for capturing gases like CO2, storing hydrogen, and purifying water, thus transforming sustainable technologies. Mary E. Brunkow, along with Frederick J. Ramsdell and Shimon Sakaguchi, won the 2025 Nobel Prize in Physiology or Medicine for discovering how the immune system prevents attacking the body, specifically identifying regulatory T cells (Tregs) and the FOXP3 gene, crucial for peripheral immune tolerance, impacting treatments for autoimmunity, transplants, and cancer. Mara Corina Machado (Peace 2025) was awarded for her long-standing efforts in promoting democratic rights in Venezuela and leading a peaceful transition movement.

questions.question: Which one of the following pairs is correctly matched?
questions.explanation: Producer of Copper - The Balaghat mines in Madhya Pradesh, Khetri mines in Rajasthan and Singhbhum district of Jharkhand. Producer of Iron ore- The Kudremukh mines located in the Western Ghats of Karnataka, Badampahar mines (Odisha), Durg-Bastar-Chandrapur belt, Maharashtra-Goa belt. Nellore Mines located in Andhra Pradesh is famous for the production of Mica.

questions.question: In August 2025, which of the following state was declared as India's first fully digitally literate state?
questions.explanation: On August 21, 2025, Kerala was declared India's first fully digitally literate state, completing the first phase of the Digi Keralam program. The initiative successfully trained 21.87 lakh previously digitally illiterate people, enabling them to use smartphones for digital calls, social media, accessing government services, and performing financial transactions.

questions.question: In August 2025, at the International Wieslaw Maniak Memorial in Poland, which Indian athlete secured a gold medal in the women's javelin throw event with a season-best effort of 62.59 meters, placing her among the top 15 globally for the season?
questions.explanation: In Athletics, India's Annu Rani has won gold medal in the women's javelin throw at the International Wieslaw Maniak Memorial in Poland with a season-best effort of 62.59 meter. The 32-year-old and Asian Games champion started strong, throwing 60.96m in her first attempt, which was already enough to secure the win. She improved further with a 62.59m throw in her second attempt and ended with another solid 60.07m throw. Turkey's Eda Tugsuz won silver with a 58.36m throw, while Australia's Lianna Davidson took bronze with 58.24m. Annu's performance places her among the top 15 women's javelin throwers in the world this season. In other events, India's Pooja placed third in the women's 800m with a time of 2:02.95. (Two minutes, two point nine five seconds) Jisna Mathew finished sixth in the women's 400m, clocking 54.12s.

questions.question: Which of the following statements is/are correct regarding Pt. Deen Dayal Upadhyaya Grameen Kaushlya Yojana (DDU-GKY)? A. It is implemented by Ministry of Rural Development B. It was launched in September 2014 C. Rural Youth in the age group of 15-35 are covered under this scheme.
questions.explanation: • Pt. Deen Dayal Upadhyaya Grameen Kaushalya Yojana (DDU-GKY) ○ Launched by: Ministry of Rural Development ○ Launch Date: September 2014 ○ Target Group: Rural poor youth, aged 15-35 years ○ Implemented under: National Rural Livelihoods Mission (NRLM) ○ Key Features: ■ Objective: Skill development and placement-linked training program for rural youth to enable them to gain employment. ■ Placement Linked: The scheme ensures that trained youth are linked to employment opportunities, enhancing their livelihoods. ■ Target Audience: Focus on youth from rural backgrounds, especially from marginalized communities. ■ Training Areas: The scheme covers a wide range of sectors including agriculture, retail, construction, hospitality, IT, and healthcare, among others. ○ Benefits: ■ Improved Employment: By providing skill training and job placements, it enhances employability among rural youth. ■ Focus on Empowerment: Aimed at improving the socio-economic status of rural youth and promoting self-reliance. ■ Skill Development Centers: Implementation through training centers across rural India, making skill development accessible.

questions.question: GDP price deflator =
questions.explanation: The GDP deflator, also called implicit price deflator, is a measure of inflation. It is the ratio of the value of goods and services an economy produces in a particular year at current prices to that of prices that prevailed during the base year. GDP price deflator = (nominal GDP ÷ real GDP) x 100

questions.question: Which of the following crops is not a cash crop or commercial crop? (a) Sugarcane (b) Cotton (c) Tobacco (d) Rice Choose from the below option:
questions.explanation: Rice is not a cash crop. The crops grown in India are categorized in four categories: - Food Grains (such as rice and wheat) , - Plantation Crops (such as tea and coffee) , - Cash Crops, and - Horticulture crops (fruits and vegetables) . Cash crops are the crops that are grown to sell for profit. Some of the most grown cash crop in India are sugarcane, tobacco, oilseeds, jute, cotton, etc.

questions.question: Which of the following Pair is not correctly matched?
questions.explanation: Yellow Revolution relates to the production of Oil Seeds, Edible Oils, mainly Mustard and Sunflower. Blue Revolution: This refers to the significant growth in fish production, particularly through aquaculture (fish farming). It aims to increase fish production to meet the growing demand for protein and to improve the livelihoods of people involved in the fishing industry. Yellow Revolution: This is associated with the increased production of oilseeds, such as mustard, groundnut, sunflower, and soybean. These oilseeds are a crucial source of edible oil in India. White Revolution: This is related to the significant increase in milk production. It was initiated to increase milk production and improve dairy farming practices in India. Silver Revolution: This is associated with the increased production of eggs and poultry. It aims to improve poultry farming and egg production to meet the growing demand for protein-rich food.

questions.question: If x + 36_________x = 12, and x > 0 then find the value of x⁴ + 324_________x³
questions.explanation: x + (36/x) = 12 put x = 6 6 + 6 = 12 [satisfied] Now, ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p21869/image005.png) ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p21869/image006.png) ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p21869/image007.png) ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p21869/image008.png)

questions.question: The population of a virus culture increases by 10% per hour for the first two hours and then decreases by 10% per hour for the next one hour. After that, the population increases by 5% per hour for the next two hours. If the initial population of the culture was 400,000 what will be the population at the end of 5 hours? (in numerical value)
questions.explanation: Initial Population: Final Population 10: 11 [First Hour, an increase of 10%] 10: 11 [Second hour, an increase of 10%] 10: 9 [Third Hour, a decrease of 10%] 20: 21 [Fourth hour, an increase of 5%] 20: 21 [Fifth Hour, an increase of 5%] (10 x 10 x 10 x 20 x 20): (11 x 11 x 9 x 21 x 21) = 400000: 480249 Final population = 480249

questions.question: Find the equation of a line passing through the point (4, -3) and parallel to the line y = 3x + 7.
questions.explanation: The slope of the given line y = 3x + 7 is 3. Since the required line is parallel to it, its slope will also be 3. Using the point-slope form of the equation of a line: y - y₁ = m(x - x₁) Substituting the values x₁ = 4, y₁ = -3 and m = 3, we get y - (-3) = 3(x - 4) y + 3 = 3x - 12 y = 3x - 15 Therefore, the equation of the required line is y = 3x - 15

questions.question: Deep, Raju and Ramu started a business. They invested various amounts in different months of the year 2016. Deep invested Rs 10000 in the month of October. Raju invested an amount which is twice the amount invested by Deep in the month of October. Ramu invested an amount in the month of July which is 50% less than the amount which was invested by Deep. Find the profit percent obtained by Ramu at the end of year 2016.
questions.explanation: Total amount invested by Deep = Rs (10000 X 3) = Rs 30000 Total amount invested by Raju = Rs (20000 X 3) = Rs 60000 Total amount invested by Ramu = Rs (5000 X 6) = Rs 30000 Therefore ratio in which profit will be divided between Deep, Raju and Ramu = 30000 : 60000 :30000 = 1 : 2 : 1 Therefore percentage of profit obtained by Ramu at the end of the year = (100 X ¼)% = 25%

questions.question: Cost price of (3p - 4) shirts is equal to the selling price of (2p + 1) shirts. If the profit percentage is 28%, then find the value of 'p'.

A big metallic sphere of radius 'A' cm is broken into 'n' number of small spheres of radius 'A/9' cm. If the total surface area of the big sphere is X sq. cm and the total surface area of all small spheres taken together is Y sq. cm, then find Y: X.
questions.explanation: Volume of big sphere = n * volume of small sphere ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p23231/image029.png) ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p23231/image030.png) ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p23231/image031.png) ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p23231/image032.png) ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p23231/image033.png)

questions.question: The lines 2x + 5y + 9 = 0 and 6x + 15y = −27 will ____________?
options: have one solution but they will not be perpendicular
questions.explanation: 6x + 15y = −27 => 6x + 15y + 27 = 0 Now, 2/6 = 5/15 = 9/27 = ⅓ So, the lines will have infinite solutions

questions.question: W is the centre of a circle, and a chord ST is extended to a point U. If UV is tangent of the circle such that ∠VUT = 46° and ∠UVT = 28°, then find ∠WSV is equal to -
questions.explanation: ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p19361/image004.jpg) In ∆TUV, ∠STV = ∠TVU + ∠VUT (Sum of two interior opposite angles is equal to exterior angle) = 46 + 28 = 74 According to the theorem of chords of a circle, the angle subtended at the centre of the circle by an arc is twice the angle subtended by it at any other point on the circumference. ∠SWV = 2 * ∠STV = 2 * 74 = 148° Since, SW = WV = radius So, ∆SWV is an isosceles triangle. ∠VSW = ∠SVW So, ∠WSV = (180 - 148)/2 = 16°

questions.question: Two chords ST and UV extended to a common point X such that UV = 8 cm, ST = 22 cm, and SX = 30 cm, then the length of the secant XU is equal to:
questions.explanation: ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p19877/image004.jpg) Let XV = a cm Intersecting Secants Theorem, XT * XS = XV * XU (30 - 22) * 30 = a * (a + 8) => 240 = a² + 8a => a² + 8a - 240 = 0 => (a - 12)(a + 20) = 0 => a = 12 or -20 (neglecting the negative value) So, a = 12 cm Therefore, XU = 8 + 12 = 20 cm

questions.question: Average of five results is 23, if both the average of first three and last three results is 20, then 3ʳᵈ number is ____?
questions.explanation: According to Question; Total results = 5 Average of five results = 23 Average of first three results = 20 = Average of last three results We know, Average of five results = (1ˢᵗ + 2ⁿᵈ + 3ʳᵈ + 4ᵗʰ +5ᵗʰ) number / 5 3ʳᵈ number = (3 x Average of first three results) +(3 x Average of last three results) - (5 x Average of five results) =3 x 20 + 3 x 20 - 5 x 23 =120-115 =5 Ans.

questions.question: Which of the following quadratic equation has equal roots?
Ram has purchased soft drink at Rs 50/liter. In what ratio water should be mixed with soft drink (water: soft drink) if he sells the mixture at Rs 40/liter and still makes a profit of 20%?
questions.explanation: By selling the mixture at Rs 40/liter he is making a profit of 20% hence cost price of the mixture = 40/1.2=Rs 33⅓ As we know that the Rs 50 is the price of 1 liter of the mixture hence Rs 33⅓ will be price of = 100/(50 X 3)=⅔ liter Hence amount of water in the mixture = 1 liter - ⅔ liter = ⅓ liter Therefore required ratio = ⅓ ∶⅔ = 1 : 2

questions.question: Three triangles are created inside from the three corners of a larger equilateral triangle in such a way that each side of each smaller triangle is one-five of the corresponding side of the larger triangle. What is the ratio of the total area of the three smaller triangles and the area of the larger triangle respectively?
questions.explanation: Let the side of the larger triangle be '5' cm Side of the smaller triangle = 5/5 = 1 cm The area of the equilateral triangle = √¾ * Side² Hence, the required ratio = (3 * √¾ * 1²)/ (√¾ * 5 * 5) = 3: 25

questions.question: If A + B + C = 0 and tan A + tan B + tan C = 0, find the value of tan ² A_________tanB.tanC + tan ² B_________tanA.tanC + tan ² C_________ tanA.tanB
questions.explanation: Given: A + B + C = 0, tan A + tan B + tan C = 0 Formula used: tan ³ A + tan ³ B + tan ³ C = 3 × tan A × tan B × tan C Calculation: tan ² A/tanB.tanC + tan ² B/tanA.tanC + tan ² C/tanA.tanB ⇒ tan ³ A + tan ³ B + tan ³ C/tanA.tanB.tanC ⇒ 3 × tanA × tanB × tanC/tanA.tanB.tanC ⇒ 3 ∴ Required answer is 3.

questions.question: O is a point inside the parallelogram WXYZ, where OY and OZ are the angle bisector of ∠Y and ∠Z respectively. If ∠YOZ = 85° and ∠W = 74°, then find the value of ∠OZW + ∠OYX.
questions.explanation: ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p19066/image028.jpg) Since ∠W = ∠Y = 74° ∠OYX = 74°/2 = 37° ∠OZY = 180° - 85° - 37° = 58° ∠OZY = ∠OZW = 58° ∠OZW + ∠OYX = 58° + 37° = 95°

questions.question: Karan wants to sell his existing mobile phone and was interested in buying a new phone. He found an awesome deal on Amazon where he sold his old phone and bought a new phone at 12% discount. Had he sold the phone at Cashify app and used the cash received to buy the same phone, then, he would have received the new phone at a discount of 20%. If the cost of new phone is 30000 (Assuming cost is same at both the places i.e., Amazon and Cashify), then, find the difference in the price offered by Amazon and Cashify?
questions.explanation: Cost of new phone = 30000 Discount offered by Amazon = 12% of 30000 = 3600 Discount offered by Cashify = 20% of 30000 = 6000 Difference = 2400

questions.question: Amit incurs a loss of 25% on selling 35 oranges for Rs.60. What is the minimum number of oranges should he sell for Rs.30 so as to make a profit of 20%?
questions.explanation: Cost price of one orange = (60/35) * (100/75) = (12/7) * (4/3) = Rs.(16/7) To make a profit of 20%, Cost price of oranges sold = 30 * 100/120 = Rs.25 Number of oranges in Rs.16/7 = 1 Number of oranges in Rs.25 = 25 * 7/16 = 10.9 = 11. (Minimum number of oranges should he sell)

questions.question: The solution of the following equations is: 3x + y - z = -1 2x - y + z = -4 7x + 5y - z = -1
questions.explanation: Instead of solving these equations, we may check the option which satisfies them. Option a: ->3 x (-1) + 1 - (-1) = -3 + 1 + 1 = -1 ->2 x (-1) - 1 + (-1) = -2 - 1 - 1 = -4 ->7 x (-1) + 5 x (1) - (-1) = -7 + 5 + 1 = -1 All three equations are satisfied. Hence option a is correct.

questions.question: What annual installment will discharge a debt of Rs. 3250 in four equal installments at the rate of 8% p.a. on simple interest annually?
Which of the following statements is/are true? Statement A: The unit digit of (13⁴¹ X 17¹⁸ X 18⁸²) is 8. Statement B: The unit digit of (25¹¹! + 23¹²!) is 4.
questions.explanation: The concept of cyclicity is used to identify the last digit of the number. For example: 2¹ = 2 2² = 4 2³ = 8 2⁴ = 16 2⁵ = 32 After four consecutive powers, unit digit is repeated. Hence, cyclicity of 2 is 4. Similarly, we can calculate cyclicity of all the numbers as in the following table Cyclicity Chart: ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p8063/image001.png) [Note: When, remainder is 0, power = cyclicity] Statement A: Unit digit of 13⁴¹ = unit digit of 3⁴¹ = unit digit of 3¹ = 3 Unit digit of 17¹⁸ = Unit digit of 7¹⁸ = Unit digit of 7² = 9 And, Unit digit of 18⁸² = Unit digit of 8⁸² = Unit digit of 8² = 4 So, unit digit (13⁴¹ X 17¹⁸ X 18⁸²) = unit digit of (3 X 9 X 4) = 8 Hence, statement A is true. Statement B: Unit digit of 25¹¹! = 5 And, unit digit of 23¹²! = unit digit of 3¹²! = unit digit of 3⁴ = 1 So, unit digit of (25¹¹! + 23¹²!) = 5 + 1 = 6 Hence, statement B is false.

questions.question: In the given figure, a square PQRS is inscribed in a quadrant PARB. If PQ = 7 cm, find the area of the shaded region. ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p21587/image015.jpg)
questions.explanation: Side of square = 7 cm Diagonal of square = 7√2 cm Hence, radius of quadrant = 7√2 cm Area of shaded region = Area of Quadrant - Area of Square = (¼ * 22/7 * 7√2 * 7√2 - 7 * 7) cm² = (77 - 49) cm² = 28 cm²

questions.question: In Δ ABC, ∠ B = 90°. If AB = √21 cm, and AC - BC = 1 cm, then find the value of sin C.
questions.explanation: ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p24040/image001.png) Let AC = X cm, then BC = X - 1 cm. By Pythagoras theorem, AC² = AB² + BC² X² = 21 + (X - 1)² X² = 21 + X² + 1 - 2X 2X = 22 X = 11 So, AC = 11 cm, and BC = 10 cm Now, sin C = AB/AC = √21/11

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom. To cut one's teeth on
options: To gain initial experience in something
questions.explanation: This idiom refers to gaining first experience but can be nuanced as "learning the hard way." Example: "She cut her teeth on complex international projects before leading her own team." Hence, option (b) is the correct answer.

questions.question: Rearrange the steps of a typical criminal trial in the correct order: P. The jury deliberates in private to reach a unanimous verdict. Q. Both the prosecution and defense present their opening statements to the jury. R. Witnesses are called and evidence is presented by both sides. S. A jury is selected from a pool of potential candidates through a process called voir dire.
questions.explanation: The correct order is c) S → Q → R → P. Here is the logical flow of a courtroom trial: (S): The trial cannot begin until the jury, the finders of fact, has been chosen. (Q): Once the jury is seated, both sides outline the case they intend to present. (R): This is the main body of the trial, where each side makes its case by presenting facts and testimony. (P): After all evidence and closing arguments are presented, the jury is tasked with making its final decision.

questions.question: Arrange the parts to form a correct logical sequence: 1) Initially, it was a small network used by the military and a few universities. 2) It has since evolved into a global platform connecting billions of people. 3) This development has fundamentally changed how we communicate, work, and access information. 4) The internet began as a government project in the United States.
questions.explanation: The correct logical sequence is a) 4-1-2-3. This sequence follows a clear historical timeline, from the internet's origin to its modern-day impact. 4): This states the origin, which is the logical starting point. 1): The word "Initially" elaborates on its early form, directly following the statement of origin. 2): This describes the growth from its small beginning to its current state. 3): This sentence describes the consequence or impact of the evolution mentioned in the previous step.

questions.question: Which spelling is correct for a system of administration with complex rules?
questions.explanation: The correct spelling is b) Bureaucracy. Bureaucracy refers to a system of government or administration, especially one managed by officials rather than elected representatives.

questions.question: Fill in the blank with an appropriate degree of comparison. Of all the technicians, she handles the delicate equipment ____________.
questions.explanation: The phrase "Of all the technicians" indicates a comparison among a group of three or more, requiring the superlative degree. The base adverb is "carefully." For adverbs ending in "-ly," the superlative is formed by adding "the most" before the adverb. · Option a) the more carefully: This is the comparative degree. · Option b) carefully: This is the positive degree and shows no comparison. · Option c) the carefulliest: This is not a word. Adverbs ending in "-ly" do not take the "-est" ending. · Option d) the most carefully: Correct. This is the proper superlative construction for the adverb "carefully." The complete sentence is: Of all the technicians, she handles the delicate equipment the most carefully. Hence, option (d) is the correct answer. Note: · The Positive Degree is used to denote the mere existence of some quality of what we speak about. It is used when no comparison is made. · The Comparative degree denotes a higher degree of quality than the positive and is used when two things are compared. · The Superlative degree indicates the highest degree of quality and is used when more than two things (or sets of things) are compared.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank. The children ___________ excitedly as the circus performers entered the arena.
questions.explanation: Let's look at the meanings of the given options. Whispered: to speak very quietly, using the breath but not the voice, so that only the person close to you can hear you. Shouted: to express strong emotions, such as anger, fear, or excitement, or to express strong opinions, in a loud voice. Sighed: to breathe out slowly and noisily, expressing tiredness, sadness, pleasure, etc. Murmured: to speak or say something very quietly. The word "shouted" best fits the context of excitement when the circus performers entered the arena. The other options do not represent an expression of excitement. Hence, option (b) is the correct answer. The complete sentence is: The children shouted excitedly as the circus performers entered the arena.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank. _____________ are going to attend the party that is organised by Mr. Morningstar at the Lux.
questions.explanation: - The correct option is (c) You and I. - Order of persons: Second person -> Third person -> First person. - For example: - You (second person), they (third person) and I/we (first person) - Here, 'you' (second person) and 'I' (first person) is the correct order in the sentence. - The other options are grammatically incorrect. - Therefore, the correct answer is: You and I are going to attend the party that is organised by Mr. Morningstar at the Lux.

questions.question: Select the option that can be used as a one-word substitute for the given group of words. The killing of a king
questions.explanation: Regicide: The act of killing a king. Homicide: The killing of a human being by another human being. Patricide: The act of killing one's own father. Fratricide: The act of killing one's own brother. Hence, option (c) is correct.

questions.question: Identify the sentence with the correct use of verb form.
options: Consumers also shifts priorities, choosing quality over quantity and supporting ethical brands that ensured fair wages and safe working conditions.
Sustainable fashion represent a broader cultural shift toward mindful consumption.
Many fashion weeks introduced sustainability-focused collections, encouraging the industry to rethink supply chains and production methods.
Circular fashion, which emphasize repairing, reusing, and recycling garments, became increasingly popular.
questions.explanation: Option (a): The subject is "Consumers," which is plural. The verb is "shifts," which is the singular form. The correct verb form would be the past tense verb "shifted" with the existing past tense verb "ensured". Option (b): The subject is "Sustainable fashion," which is a singular concept. The verb is "represent," which is the plural form. The correct verb form would be "represents" (singular verb). Option (c): The subject is "Many fashion weeks," which is plural. The verb is "introduced," which is in the simple past tense. The simple past tense (except for the verb 'to be') has the same form for both singular and plural subjects, so the agreement is correct. The sentence is grammatically sound. Option (d): The error is in the relative clause "which emphasize repairing...". The subject of this clause is the pronoun "which," which refers to "Circular fashion." Since "Circular fashion" is singular, the verb must also be singular. "Emphasize" is the plural form; the correct form is "emphasizes" (singular). Hence, option (c) is the correct answer.

questions.question: What is the best meaning of "persist" in the sentence: "While challenges like higher costs and limited availability persist, the movement demonstrated that conscious choices could reduce the environmental footprint of the fashion industry"?
options: Continue to exist or endure over time
Disappear quickly without leaving any impact
Solve a problem completely and permanently
questions.explanation: Here, "persist" means that these challenges continue to exist or endure over time, despite efforts to address them. Hence, option (a) is the correct answer.

questions.question: What is a major challenge mentioned in the passage for the growth of sustainable fashion?
options: High costs and limited availability of eco-friendly options
questions.explanation: The passage [in para 1] mentions, "While challenges like higher costs and limited availability persist, the movement demonstrated that conscious choices could reduce the environmental footprint..." Option (a): This is incorrect. The passage suggests the opposite, that "conscious choices" by consumers are driving the movement, indicating a growing interest, not a limited one. Option (b): This is correct. The passage directly lists "higher costs and limited availability" as challenges that "persist" for the sustainable fashion movement. Option (c): This is incorrect. The passage mentions recycling as part of a solution (circular fashion), not as a problem of overproduction. Option (d): This is incorrect. The passage doesn't mention a lack of trends. In fact, it suggests sustainability is becoming a trend itself by noting that "fashion weeks introduced sustainability-focused collections." Hence, option (b) is the correct answer.

questions.question: What does the passage suggest about the future of the fashion industry?
options: Fast fashion will completely replace sustainable practices
Fashion cannot be both stylish and environmentally responsible
Ethical production has no influence on fashion trends
Conscious consumer choices can help reduce the industry's environmental impact
questions.explanation: The passage [in para 1] describes sustainable fashion as a growing "movement" and a "broader cultural shift." It highlights positive actions like the rise of "circular fashion" and consumers "supporting ethical brands." The concluding sentence of the implied passage states that "conscious choices could reduce the environmental footprint of the fashion industry." Option (a): This is incorrect. The passage suggests the opposite, that sustainable practices are on the rise and are challenging the dominance of fast fashion. Option (b): This is incorrect. The passage mentions that "many fashion weeks introduced sustainability-focused collections," implying that the height of fashion is embracing sustainability, thus combining style with responsibility. Option (c): This is incorrect. The passage states that consumers are "supporting ethical brands," which directly influences what becomes popular and trendy in the market. Option (d): This is correct. This statement is the central argument of the passage. The text explicitly links the "movement" to "conscious choices" and states that these choices can lead to a positive environmental outcome. Hence, option (d) is the correct answer.

questions.question: What type of pronoun is the word "which" in the sentence: "Circular fashion, which emphasizes repairing, reusing, and recycling garments, became increasingly popular"?
questions.explanation: The word "which" introduces the clause, which emphasizes repairing, reusing, and recycling garments." This clause provides additional information about the noun that comes before it, "Circular fashion." A relative pronoun is used to connect a clause (a relative clause) to a noun or pronoun. It "relates" the extra information back to the subject. The most common relative pronouns are who, whom, whose, which, and that. In this sentence, "which" is doing exactly that, i.e., connecting the descriptive clause to "Circular fashion." Hence, option (a) is the correct answer. Option (b): A possessive pronoun shows ownership (e.g., mine, yours, his). "Which" does not indicate ownership here. Option (c): A demonstrative pronoun points to a specific noun (e.g., this, that, these). "Which" is not pointing something out in this context. Option (d): An interrogative pronoun is used to ask a question (e.g., "Which one do you want?"). The sentence is a statement, not a question.

questions.question: The following sentence has been split into four segments. Identify the segment that contains a grammatical error. The government has refuse to allow foreign journalists into the area for several weeks.
questions.explanation: The base verb 'refuse' should be replaced with the third form of verb 'refused' to form the correct grammatical structure of the present perfect tense: subject + has/have + v3 + object. So, there's an error in the segment in option (a). The correct sentence is: The government has refused to allow foreign journalists into the area for several weeks. Hence, option (a) is the correct answer.

questions.question: Select the option that will improve the underlined part of the given sentence. In case no improvement is needed, select 'No improvement required'. Movement of stock prices can classify into only two groups, namely bullish and bearish.
questions.explanation: The given sentence suggests that 'movement of stock prices' is the doer of the action, which is not meaningful. It should be ‘can be classified into’ which is the passive voice form (can + be + v3), indicating that action is done upon the 'movement'. Options (b) and (c) are incorrect as they do not follow a proper grammatical structure. Hence, option (d) is the answer. Note: Active and Passive Voice: When the person doing the action is the subject, the sentence is said to be in active form/voice. When the subject is what the action is directed at, then the sentence is said to be in passive form/voice. For example: Active Voice: Rita and her friends performed a skit. Here 'Rita and her friends' (subject) perform the action. Passive Voice: A skit was performed by Rita and her friends. Here 'skit' is the subject (at which the action is directed).

questions.question: Choose the sentence that uses a homonym of the underlined word: Please turn off the light when you leave the room.
options: The lamp provided enough light to read comfortably.
The suitcase felt surprisingly light because it was empty.
Scientists study how light travels in a vacuum.
A sudden flash of light startled the deer.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is b) The suitcase felt surprisingly light because it was empty. In the given sentence, light is a noun meaning "electromagnetic radiation that makes things visible" (illumination). In option b), light is an adjective meaning "having little weight; not heavy." This is a homonym (same spelling, different meaning). Options a), c), and d) all use "light" with the same meaning as the original sentence (illumination).

questions.question: Which among the following sentences uses the article (if required) before the word 'fourth' correctly?
options: Please turn to a fourth chapter of the book.
Please turn to the fourth chapter of the book.
Please turn to an fourth chapter of the book.
Please turn to fourth chapter of the book.
questions.explanation: Option (a): The indefinite article "a" is used for a non-specific item. Since "fourth" pinpoints one specific chapter, using "a" is incorrect. Option (b): Correct. The ordinal number "fourth" identifies a specific chapter in the sequence. The definite article "the" is required to refer to this specific, unique position. Option (c): This has two errors. Like option (a), it incorrectly uses an indefinite article ("an") where the definite article ("the") is needed. "An" is used before vowel sounds. The word "fourth" begins with a consonant "f" sound, so even if an indefinite article were appropriate, it would be "a," not "an." Option (d): In English, an ordinal number modifying a noun almost always needs the definite article "the" before it. Omitting the article makes the phrase grammatically incomplete. Hence, option (b) is the answer. Note: Definite and indefinite articles: In the English language, we have one definite article 'the'; it refers to or introduces, a particular, specific noun. 'An' and 'a' are indefinite articles; they refer to, or introduce, an unspecified noun.

questions.question: Select the sentence that uses the given idiom correctly. Hit the nail on the head
options: She hit the nail on the head when she was hammering the nails into the wall.
I hit the nail on the head when I failed to throw the ball at the target.
They hit the nail on the head when they could not cook a delicious meal.
She hit the nail on the head when she guessed the correct answer in the trivia game.
questions.explanation: The idiom "hit the nail on the head" means to describe something that is exactly right or accurate, especially in terms of an opinion, statement, or action. Among the given options, option (d) correctly uses the idiom to describe guessing the correct answer in a trivia game, indicating that the person's guess was exactly right. Hence, (d) is the correct answer. The rest of the options use the idiom incorrectly.

questions.question: The following sentence has been split into four segments. Identify the segment that contains a grammatical error. Neither the managers nor the owner were surprised at the quarterly performance.
questions.explanation: When two subjects are connected using 'neither...nor', the verb is used according to the nearest subject. In the given sentence, the verb should be used according to the subject 'owner' which is singular in nature. So, the plural verb 'were' should be replaced with its singular verb 'was' to rectify the given sentence. Therefore, the error is in the segment in option (b). Hence, option (b) is the correct answer. The correct sentence is: Neither the managers nor the owner was surprised at the quarterly performance. NOTE/- Subject-Verb Agreement: Matching up singular or plural subjects with singular or plural forms of a verb is part of the process called agreement. This can be understood better by the examples given: - "She likes cooking." [singular subject and verb] -- "They like to cook vegetables." [plural subject and verb]

questions.question: Identify the word that doesn't fit the part of speech needed. The weather was extremely wind on the coast.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is c) wind. The word "wind" is a noun/verb. After the verb "was" and the adverb "extremely," an adjective is needed to describe the subject "weather." The adjective form of "wind" is "windy." The corrected sentence would be: "The weather was extremely windy on the coast." Option (a): "weather" is a noun. It correctly serves as the subject of the sentence. Option (b): "extremely" is an adverb of degree. It is used here to intensify the word that follows it, which should be an adjective. Its use is correct. Option (d): "coast" is a noun. It is correctly used as the object of the preposition "on." The prepositional phrase "on the coast" acts as an adverbial phrase of place.

questions.question: She made an inadvertent mistake by deleting the important client file. What is the SYNONYM of 'inadvertent'?
questions.explanation: "Inadvertent" means not resulting from or achieved through deliberate planning; accidental. The word closest in meaning is "unintentional", which means not done on purpose. Option (a) Deliberate: This is an antonym, meaning done consciously and intentionally. Option (c) Obvious: This describes something that is easily perceived or understood, which is a different quality of the mistake. Option (d) Costly: This describes the consequence of the mistake (having a high price), not its nature. Hence, option (b) is the correct answer.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate antonym of the underlined word in the following sentence. The detective was determined to uncover the clandestine activities of the criminal organisation.
questions.explanation: Clandestine: Kept secret or done secretly, often because it is illegal or unauthorised. Covert: not openly acknowledged or displayed. Overt: done or shown openly; plainly or readily apparent. Hidden: kept out of sight; concealed. Surreptitious: kept secret, especially because it would not be approved of. Among the given options, the word 'overt' is opposite in meaning to the word 'clandestine'. So, the two words can be used as antonyms of each other. Hence, (b) is the correct answer.

questions.question: Select the option that can be used as a one-word substitute for the given group of words. A remedy for all diseases or problems
questions.explanation: Elixir: A magical or medicinal potion believed to cure ailments or grant immortality, but not necessarily for all problems. Panacea: A solution or remedy that claims to solve all difficulties or cure all diseases. Nostrum: A medicine, especially one that is not considered effective, prepared by an unqualified person. Antidote: A medicine taken to counteract a specific poison or toxin. So, "a remedy for all diseases or problems" is best described as "panacea". Hence, option (b) is the correct answer.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate synonym of the given word. Platitude
questions.explanation: Platitude: A remark or statement, especially one with a moral content, that has been used too often to be interesting or thoughtful. Cliché: A phrase or opinion that is overused and betrays a lack of original thought. Axiom: A statement or proposition that is regarded as being established, accepted, or self-evidently true. Originality: The quality of being new and inventive. Innovation: The action or process of innovating (introducing new ideas or methods). The synonym closest in meaning is "Cliché" (also "truism," "banality," or "bromide"). Hence, option (c) is the answer.

questions.question: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in direct speech. The child told his mother that he had just finished the whole packet of chips.
options: The child said to his mother, "I had just finished the whole packet of chips."
The child said to his mother, "I will just finish the whole packet of chips."
The child said to his mother, "I had to finish the whole packet of chips."
The child said to his mother, "I will have to finish the whole packet of chips."
questions.explanation: The sentence in the Indirect Speech is in Past Perfect. Therefore, the sentence in the Direct Speech will also be in Past Perfect. Past Perfect (Subject + had + Past Participle(V3) + Object) will be converted into the same tense form. For example: He said that he had just finished the movie. (Indirect Speech) He said, "I had just finished the movie" (Direct Speech) The following things in the sentence will change when changed from Indirect Speech to Direct Speech: Indirect SpeechDirect Speech told... thatSaid to heI Therefore, the correct answer is: (a) The child said to his mother, "I had just finished the whole packet of chips."
